response.justification: The role demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, strong SQL Server/Oracle skills, and fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. The candidate is a capable full-stack engineer (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) with strong testing and craftsmanship habits, but C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a university thesis — far short of the requirement. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience and no fixed-income or investment-banking domain work; the RIDE Capital role was investment-adjacent but built on JavaScript/GraphQL, not C++ or financial-product systems. The mandatory and domain criteria are not met.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Some professional C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Excellent communication evidenced by talks, workshops, and fluent English
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months plus a thesis, not the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server/Oracle; primary work is on Linux, not Windows
No fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience
